Bash(gh pr create --title "Prevent duplicate topic creation warnings in integration specs" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Reorder 17 integration specs to call `draw_routes` \(with default `create_topics: true`\) **before** `produce`/`produce_many`, preventing Kafka `TOPIC_ALREADY_EXISTS` warnings that fail `verify_kafka_warnings`
- Make `SPEC_HASH` in `DataCollector` use the relative path from the repo root instead of `$PROGRAM_NAME` \(absolute path\), ensuring consistent topic-to-spec tracing across local dev and CI environments

## Background

Several integration specs were producing messages before calling `draw_routes\(create_topics: false\)`. This caused Kafka's auto-topic-creation to handle topic creation. When consumers later issued metadata requests, the broker would attempt auto-creation again, logging `TOPIC_ALREADY_EXISTS` warnings:

```
WARN Auto topic creation failed for it-394046-cb0f612eb21e with error 'TOPIC_ALREADY_EXISTS'
```

The fix reorders each spec so topics are explicitly created via admin API \(`draw_routes` with default `create_topics: true`\) before any messages are produced.

## Test plan
- [ ] CI integration tests pass without `TOPIC_ALREADY_EXISTS` warnings
- [ ] `verify_kafka_warnings` passes cleanly
EOF
\)")

Bash(gh pr create --title "Fix TOPIC_ALREADY_EXISTS warning in VP patterned integration test" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- The `vp/from_earliest_patterned_spec` was causing a `TOPIC_ALREADY_EXISTS` Kafka warning detected by `verify_kafka_warnings` in CI \([failing job]\(https://github.com/karafka/karafka/actions/runs/23698783361/job/69038647530\)\)
- The race occurred because `produce_many` triggered Kafka auto-topic-creation, and then the pattern subscription's metadata refresh also triggered auto-creation for the same topic
- Fix: explicitly create the topic via `Karafka::Admin.create_topic` before producing, consistent with how `multiplexing/with_patterns_spec` handles this

## Test plan
- [ ] CI `integrations_pro` job passes without `TOPIC_ALREADY_EXISTS` warnings
EOF
\)")

Bash(git add lib/karafka/pro/swarm/liveness_listener.rb lib/karafka/instrumentation/vendors/kubernetes/liveness_listener.rb && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Make liveness listeners fiber-safe \(#3085\)

Replace Thread.current.object_id with Fiber.current.object_id for keying
polling and consumption timestamp hashes. Multiple fibers on the same
thread share a thread object_id, causing them to overwrite each other's
timestamps. Each fiber has a unique object_id, and in non-fiber contexts
the root fiber provides the same uniqueness guarantees as before.
EOF
\)")

Bash(gh pr create --title "Make liveness listeners fiber-safe" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Closes #3085

## Summary
- Replace `Thread.current.object_id` with `Fiber.current.object_id` in both liveness listeners for keying `@pollings` and `@consumptions` hashes
- Multiple fibers on the same thread share a thread `object_id`, causing them to overwrite each other's timestamps and break health monitoring
- Each fiber has a unique `object_id`, and in non-fiber contexts the root fiber provides the same uniqueness guarantees as before

## Affected files
- `lib/karafka/pro/swarm/liveness_listener.rb` — swarm supervision liveness
- `lib/karafka/instrumentation/vendors/kubernetes/liveness_listener.rb` — Kubernetes liveness

## Test plan
- [x] Existing Kubernetes liveness listener specs pass
- [ ] Verify swarm liveness listener in Pro mode
- [ ] Verify fiber-based workloads no longer see timestamp collisions in liveness tracking
EOF
\)")

Bash(git checkout -b fix/stabilize-cooperative-rebalance-transaction-spec && git add spec/integrations/pro/rebalancing/transactions/revoke_reclaim_continuity_cooperative_spec.rb && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Reduce max_messages to stabilize cooperative rebalance transaction spec

Lower max_messages from 1_000 to 100 in the cooperative-sticky
transactional rebalance integration spec. This narrows the time window
where a transaction can be in-flight during a rebalance, reducing the
likelihood of an illegal_generation error causing a flaky failure.
EOF

Bash(gh pr create --title "Stabilize cooperative rebalance transaction integration spec" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Reduce `max_messages` from 1,000 to 100 in `revoke_reclaim_continuity_cooperative_spec.rb`
- This narrows the time window where a transaction can be in-flight during a cooperative-sticky rebalance, reducing the likelihood of an `illegal_generation` error causing a flaky failure
- The spec validates the same behavior \(offset and data continuity across transactional rebalances\) — only the batch size per consume call changes

## Context
The spec occasionally fails \(~0.2%\) when a rebalance triggered by the second consumer lands mid-transaction. The broker rejects the transactional offset commit with `ILLEGAL_GENERATION` because the generation ID changed. Smaller batches mean shorter transactions and a narrower race window.

## Test plan
- [ ] CI passes on the `integrations_pro \(4.0.0, 1\)` job that previously failed
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Fix swarm liveness reporting gaps and rename for clarity

- Add orphaned node detection to Pro swarm liveness listener \(was missing
  from report_status, unlike the base class report_liveness\)
- Report liveness on connection.listener.before_fetch_loop in both OSS
  and Pro listeners so nodes send an initial healthy report before the
  first consumption starts
- Rename stop_if_not_responding to stop_if_not_reporting and
  NOT_RESPONDING_SHUTDOWN_STATUS to NOT_REPORTING_SHUTDOWN_STATUS for
  clarity
EOF
\)")

Bash(gh pr create --title "Fix swarm liveness reporting gaps and improve naming" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- **Fix**: Add orphaned node detection to Pro swarm liveness listener. The Pro `report_status` was missing the `Kernel.exit!\(orphaned_exit_code\) if node.orphaned?` check that the base `report_liveness` has, meaning orphaned Pro nodes would keep running indefinitely instead of exiting.
- **Fix**: Report liveness on `connection.listener.before_fetch_loop` in both OSS and Pro listeners. Without this, if the first consumption takes longer than `node_report_timeout`, the supervisor would kill the node with status `-1` \(not reporting\) because no initial healthy report was sent before consumption started.
- **Maintenance**: Rename `stop_if_not_responding` → `stop_if_not_reporting` and `NOT_RESPONDING_SHUTDOWN_STATUS` → `NOT_REPORTING_SHUTDOWN_STATUS` for clarity — the method checks whether the node stopped *reporting*, not whether it stopped *responding*.

## Test plan
- [x] Unit test for orphaned detection in Pro liveness listener
- [x] Unit tests for `on_connection_listener_before_fetch_loop` in both OSS and Pro listeners
- [x] Integration test: Pro orphaned node detection triggers exit and restart
- [x] Integration test: OSS node survives long first consumption thanks to before_fetch_loop report
- [x] Integration test: Pro node survives long first consumption thanks to before_fetch_loop report
EOF
\)")

Bash(gh pr edit 3097 --title "Fix swarm liveness reporting gaps and improve naming" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- **Fix**: Add orphaned node detection to Pro swarm liveness listener. The Pro `report_status` was missing the `Kernel.exit!\(orphaned_exit_code\) if node.orphaned?` check that the base `report_liveness` has, meaning orphaned Pro nodes would keep running indefinitely instead of exiting.
- **Fix**: Report liveness on `connection.listener.before_fetch_loop` in both OSS and Pro listeners. Without this, if the first consumption takes longer than `node_report_timeout`, the supervisor would kill the node with status `-1` \(not reporting\) because no initial healthy report was sent before consumption started.
- **Maintenance**: Rename `stop_if_not_responding` → `stop_if_not_reporting` and `NOT_RESPONDING_SHUTDOWN_STATUS` → `NOT_REPORTING_SHUTDOWN_STATUS` for clarity — the method checks whether the node stopped *reporting*, not whether it stopped *responding*.

## Test plan
- [x] Unit test for orphaned detection in Pro liveness listener
- [x] Unit tests for `on_connection_listener_before_fetch_loop` in both OSS and Pro listeners
- [x] Integration test: Pro orphaned node detection triggers exit and restart
- [x] Integration test: OSS node survives long first consumption thanks to before_fetch_loop report
- [x] Integration test: Pro node survives long first consumption thanks to before_fetch_loop report
EOF
\)")

Bash(git checkout master && git checkout -b fix/swarm-liveness-wait-pinging)

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Fire liveness events during wait_pinging for shutdown with active LRJ

When a node with an active LRJ job shuts down, the listener exits the
fetch loop and enters wait_pinging. Previously wait_pinging did not fire
any instrumentation events, so the liveness listener stopped reporting
entirely — making the node go silent during shutdown.

Add @events_poller.call to wait_pinging so that client.events_poll fires,
allowing both OSS and Pro liveness listeners to continue reporting health
status. The IntervalRunner throttles calls to the configured tick interval.
EOF
\)")
Bash(git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master)

Bash(gh pr create --draft --title "Fix liveness reporting during shutdown with active LRJ jobs" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## 💪 What

- Adds `@events_poller.call` to `wait_pinging` in `Listener` so `client.events_poll` fires during the shutdown wait phase.
- Fixes silent nodes: both OSS and Pro liveness listeners continue reporting health status while the listener waits for active LRJ jobs to finish.
- Adds integration test verifying liveness reports are emitted during the `wait_pinging` phase.

## 🤔 Why

- When a node with an active LRJ job shuts down, the listener exits the fetch loop and enters `wait_pinging`. This method didn't fire any instrumentation events, so the liveness listener stopped reporting entirely — making the node go silent.
- The supervisor cannot distinguish a healthy node doing shutdown work from a stuck one when liveness reports stop.
- The `IntervalRunner` already throttles `@events_poller.call` to the configured tick interval \(~5s\), so adding it to the 0.2s sleep loop has no performance impact.

## 👩‍🔬 How to validate

1. Start a swarm with an LRJ consumer that takes a long time to process
2. Send a shutdown signal while the LRJ job is active
3. Observe that liveness reports continue during the shutdown wait phase \(previously they stopped entirely\)

Or run the integration test:
```
bundle exec rspec spec/integrations/pro/swarm/with_liveness_lrj_shutdown_not_reporting_spec.rb
```
EOF
\)")

Bash(git add spec/integrations/pro/swarm/with_liveness_lrj_shutdown_not_reporting_spec.rb && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Reduce LRJ sleep from 120s to 30s in liveness wait_pinging test

30s is more than enough for the node to stay in wait_pinging while the
main process verifies liveness reports. Also clean up stale comments.
EOF
\)" && git push)

Bash(git add spec/integrations/pro/swarm/with_liveness_lrj_shutdown_not_reporting_spec.rb && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Rewrite liveness wait_pinging test to use supervisor-side verification

Instead of detecting client.events_poll in the child process via pipe IPC
\(unreliable across platforms\), test from the supervisor's perspective:

1. Send TERM directly to the node \(not the supervisor\) so it enters
   wait_pinging while the supervisor's control loop keeps monitoring
2. If the node stops reporting, stop_if_not_reporting fires with status -1
3. Assert no NOT_REPORTING_SHUTDOWN_STATUS events occurred

This eliminates cross-process event subscription issues that caused
flaky failures on both Linux and macOS.
EOF
\)")

Bash(git add lib/karafka/connection/listener.rb && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Rescue Rdkafka::RdkafkaError around events_poller in wait_pinging

wait_pinging is a shutdown/quiet helper where rdkafka polling errors are
irrelevant. Without this rescue, a RdkafkaError from events_poll would
bubble into fetch_loop's rescue/retry path and trigger a full listener
reset during shutdown.
EOF
\)" && git push)

Bash(BUNDLE_GEMFILE=Gemfile.lint bundle exec rubocop lib/karafka/connection/client.rb lib/karafka/connection/listener.rb lib/karafka/helpers/interval_runner.rb spec/lib/karafka/helpers/interval_runner_spec.rb spec/lib/karafka/connection/client_spec.rb 2>&1 | grep -E "offense|inspecting" -i)

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Add safe flag to events_poll and forward args through IntervalRunner

- IntervalRunner#call now uses \(...\) to forward arguments to the block
- Client#events_poll accepts safe: keyword to rescue RdkafkaError during
  shutdown/quiet paths instead of propagating it to the listener reset
- Listener's events_poller block forwards **opts to events_poll
- wait_pinging uses @events_poller.call\(safe: true\) instead of
  begin/rescue wrapper
- Add tests for IntervalRunner argument forwarding and events_poll safe flag
EOF
\)" && git push)

Fix flaky encryption spec by using full element as chunk

The previous chunk was just "non" \(3 bytes\), which could randomly
appear in encrypted binary payloads causing false assertion failures.
Use the full plaintext element instead.
EOF
\)" && git push)

Allow replica state warning in Kafka log checker

The warning "The replica state of replica ID doesn't exist in the leader
node" occurs transiently during topic deletion and is benign.
EOF
\)" && git push)

Bash(git add spec/integrations/consumption/eofed/post_consumption_spec.rb && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Fix flaky eofed post_consumption integration spec

The spec assumed EOF would always arrive in the same poll as messages,
making eofed? true inside #consume. Under CI timing, EOF can arrive in
a separate poll, triggering the #eofed callback which contained a raise,
crashing the test. Handle both valid delivery paths by setting DT[:eof]
in #eofed as well.
EOF
\)")

Bash(gh pr create --title "Fix flaky eofed post_consumption integration spec" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Fix flaky `consumption/eofed/post_consumption_spec.rb` that intermittently fails on CI \([example failure]\(https://github.com/karafka/karafka/actions/runs/24060047195/job/70174290508\)\)
- The spec assumed EOF would always arrive in the same poll as messages, but under CI timing EOF can arrive in a separate poll, triggering the `#eofed` callback which contained `raise`
- Handle both valid EOF delivery paths \(with messages in `#consume` or separately via `#eofed`\) by setting `DT[:eof] = eofed?` in both methods, following the same pattern used in `eof_offset_continuity_spec.rb` and `post_eof_state_change_spec.rb`

## Test plan
- [x] Verify the spec logic: both `#consume` and `#eofed` now set `DT[:eof]`, so the wait condition `DT[:eof] == true` is satisfied regardless of which path delivers EOF
- [ ] CI integration tests pass without the flaky failure
EOF
\)")

Bash(git add spec/integrations/pro/consumption/strategies/ftr/event_filter_alternating_batches_spec.rb && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Fix flaky event_filter_alternating_batches integration spec

Guard against empty messages array in EventFilter#apply!. When
VpStabilizer runs first and clears the batch \(fewer than 10 messages
polled\), EventFilter receives an empty array and crashes on
messages.first.offset \(NoMethodError: undefined method 'offset' for
nil:NilClass\).
EOF
\)")

Bash(gh pr create --title "Fix flaky event_filter_alternating_batches spec" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Guard against empty messages array in `EventFilter#apply!` in the integration spec
- When `VpStabilizer` \(min: 10\) runs first and fewer than 10 messages are polled, it clears the messages array. The `EventFilter` then crashes on `messages.first.offset` because `first` returns `nil`
- Adding `return if messages.empty?` prevents the `NoMethodError`

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify the `ftr/event_filter_alternating_batches_spec` no longer fails intermittently in CI
EOF
\)")

Bash(ruby -e "
require 'karafka/setup/attributes_map'
result = Karafka::Setup::AttributesMap.generate
puts '=== CONSUMER ==='
result[:consumer].each { |s| puts s }
puts '=== PRODUCER ==='
result[:producer].each { |s| puts s }
")

Bash(bundle exec ruby -e "
require 'open-uri'
require 'set'
require 'rdkafka'

source = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/confluentinc/librdkafka/v' + Rdkafka::LIBRDKAFKA_VERSION + '/CONFIGURATION.md'
puts 'Fetching: ' + source
attributes = { consumer: Set.new, producer: Set.new }

URI.parse\(source\).open.readlines.each do |line|
  next unless line.include?\('|'\)
  attribute, attribute_type = line.split\('|'\).map\(&:strip\)
  case attribute_type
  when 'C'
    attributes[:consumer] << attribute
  when 'P'
    attributes[:producer] << attribute
  when '*'
    attributes[:consumer] << attribute
    attributes[:producer] << attribute
  else
    next
  end
end

attributes.transform_values!\(&:sort\)
attributes.each_value { |vals| vals.map!\(&:to_sym\) }

puts '=== CONSUMER ==='
attributes[:consumer].each { |s| puts s }
puts ''
puts '=== PRODUCER ==='
attributes[:producer].each { |s| puts s }
")
